result: I’d dissolve the illusion that anyone’s well-being can be separated from everyone else’s. Not just as a moral sentiment, but as a built-in, neurological, economic and ecological fact. Overnight we’d wake up with the same brains and bodies, but the feedback loops would be undeniable: every calorie of food, every idea, every bullet, every whispered kindness would ping through the network and come back as profit or poison to the sender, usually within a day. No external enforcement required—just perfect, personal, real-time accounting. Watch how fast the “externalities” vanish when they’re re-internalised by the only psyche you can’t escape: your own.

No external enforcement required—just perfect, personal, real-time accounting. Watch how fast the “externalities” vanish when they’re re-internalised by the only psyche you can’t escape: your own.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
